title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: ConsTree (ConsTree, GPL ≥ 3) front-end consensus-tree toolkit built TreeTools, remains engine methods wraps (strict, majority). Several split- triplet-based methods R/C++ ports asymptotically-efficient C++ FACT, FACT2 FDCT_new (Jesper Jansson et al.; used permission). Cloned reference C++ lives dev/reference/ — read porting validating; never ship .

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
code: ConsTree/ ├── R/ │   ├── ConsTree-package.R   # package doc + Rdpack glue │   ├── wrappers.R           # Strict(), Majority(), MajorityRule() → TreeTools::Consensus │   ├── selection.R          # split-selection family + shared .PoolSplits machinery │   ├── adams.R              # Adams()  (refinement to fixpoint) │   ├── local.R              # Local()  → src/local_consensus.cpp │   ├── rstar.R              # RStar()  → src/rstar.cpp │   ├── Quartet.R            # Quartet() → src/Quartet.cpp │   ├── Average.R            # distance-based average consensus (Lapointe & Cucumel) │   ├── BHV.R                # BHV geodesic distance / Fréchet mean → src/bhv.cpp │   └── RcppExports.R        # generated; never edit ├── src/                     # Rcpp C++ (no Boost in shipped code) ├── tests/testthat/          # property + idempotence + ecosystem cross-checks ├── vignettes/ConsTree.Rmd   # introductory worked-examples vignette ├── inst/REFERENCES.bib      # Rdpack bibliography (cite via \insertCite{Key}{ConsTree}) ├── inst/WORDLIST            # spelling::spell_check_package() allow-list ├── man/                     # roxygen-generated; never edit by hand └── dev/                     # NOT shipped (.Rbuildignore: ^dev$); oracle + reference C++

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: Every split-based method shares one validated pipeline R/selection.R. Reuse new split-based methods — selection rule differs. splits — distinct splits (Splits object); members — .logical(distinct), (nSplit × nTip) logical matrix; counts — occurrence count distinct split (integer); membership[[]] — indices (splits) splits tree ; labels, nTree, firstTree; trivial — short-circuit tree degenerate cases (single phylo, < 2 trees, < 4 leaves). non-NULL, return immediately. .CompatibilityMatrix(prep) — pairwise split-compatibility matrix, .matrix(CompatibleSplits(prep$splits, prep$splits)). negation conflict matrix used MajorityPlus() / Frequency(). Selection — method-specific rule produces logical keep prep$splits. .SelectedConsensus(keep, prep) — rebuild via .phylo(.Splits(members[keep, ], tipLabels = labels)) root match first input tree (TreeTools::Consensus() ).

what: Gotcha: Splits is an S4 class
title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: Single-bracket [ subsetting drops class. subset, round-trip logical matrix: .Splits(prep$members[keep, , drop = FALSE], tipLabels = prep$labels). Never splits[keep].

what: Gotcha: as.phylo is the ape generic
title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: TreeTools registers S3 methods export .phylo. Use ape::.phylo(...) code examples (dispatches TreeTools method Splits/TreeTools objects TreeTools namespace loaded).

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: Local() runtime caveat. Exact-exponential. ≤20-leaf guard bounds memory, runtime also depends input congruence — incongruent type = "induced" can intractable even n ≤ 20. call interruptible (Ctrl-C).

what: Open decisions (unresolved — surface to the user, don’t silently pick)
title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: Local() citation: currently \insertCite{JanssonShenSung2016} likely wrong MinRLC/MinILC. Confirm correct reference release. Local() hard runtime guard: none yet (interruptible) — decide whether add one.

what: The consensus lattice (invariants the tests enforce)
title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: tests/testthat/test-selection.R asserts via order-independent split-string sets (splitSet() helper). Idempotence — Method(list(t, t, t)) returns t — holds every method.
code: strict ⊆ loose  ⊆ greedy strict ⊆ majority ⊆ greedy majority ⊆ majorityPlus majority ⊆ frequency ⊆ greedy majority ∥ loose            (incomparable)

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: Functions BigCamelCase; variables camelCase; private helpers dot-prefixed (.PoolSplits). One exported method per concept; keep public API reachable ConsTree::<Method>(). @importFrom per roxygen block — never blanket @import. # Return: comment marks returned value instead explicit return(); early return() guard clauses fine (TreeTools precedent). Prefer TreeTools ape tree manipulation (NTip, Preorder, RootTree, KeepTip, RenumberTips, …); use ape:: equivalent (ape::.phylo, ape::read.tree, distance/NJ builders). Full test coverage every new/changed line — happy path, error branches, edge cases (single tree, < 4 leaves, identical trees, conflicting splits). codecov gates PR. Cite \insertCite{Key}{ConsTree} + @references \insertAllCited{}; add BibTeX inst/REFERENCES.bib (clean AuthorYear keys). Add legitimate technical terms flagged spelling::spell_check_package() inst/WORDLIST. user-visible change: update NEWS.md bump .900X dev suffix DESCRIPTION.

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: devtools::load_all() validation — install isolated library test (avoids DLL/namespace surprises; matches oracle driver loads package). PowerShell aliases R Invoke-History; always call R.exe / Rscript.exe. task complete relevant tests pass (release) R CMD check clean. Prefer GHA full R CMD check (see ../AGENTS.md). One accepted NOTE: read_symbols_from_dll (R-devel tooling, package bug). phangorn allCompat cross-check gated behind CONSENSUS_PHANGORN_TESTS=1 — R-devel/phangorn ABI mismatch can SIGSEGV (tryCatch() catch), must opt-rather wrapped.
code: # Install to an isolated library (from the package root) R.exe CMD INSTALL --library=.agent-cons .  # Regenerate Rd after any roxygen change Rscript.exe -e "roxygen2::roxygenise()"  # Run the suite / a single file against the isolated library Rscript.exe -e ".libPaths(c('.agent-cons', .libPaths())); testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat')" Rscript.exe -e ".libPaths(c('.agent-cons', .libPaths())); testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test-selection.R')"

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: dev/oracle/ shells patched FACT/FACT2/FDCT binaries diff ported method original C++ shared random fixtures — strongest check available. dev/oracle/oracle.R — FactConsensus(trees, method, rooted) writes FACT-dialect NEXUS (integer labels, literal translate line), invokes fact.exe stdin (file rule rooted), parses integer-labelled Newick back phylo. FACT_RULE maps method → algorithm bitmask: strict=1, majority=8, greedy=32, loose=128, majorityPlus=256, adams=1024. dev/oracle/check-oracle.R — cross-validation driver (Rscript.exe dev/oracle/check-oracle.R). Per-method harnesses also live dev/oracle/{freqdiff,local,rstar}/. oracle binaries untracked (dev/ .Rbuildignored absent worktrees) — run oracle main checkout.

title: ConsTree — Agent Instructions
text: FACT’s generation-path uses POSIX bits absent MinGW; patched build drops : remove #include <sys/resource.h> set_stack, map rand_r(&seed_) → rand(), #define M_PI (compile -D_USE_MATH_DEFINES). consensus paths untouched. FACT2/FDCT additionally need boost::dynamic_bitset headers build time — fine dev oracle, never shipped package.

title: Summarizing tree samples with ConsTree
text: split-selection methods differ groupings keep, form nested sequence increasing resolution. see , take seven trees share backbone disagree placement leaves: method retains superset groupings kept one :  Strict() keeps two groupings present every tree; Majority() adds half; Frequency() keeps grouping beats every grouping conflicts ; Greedy() adds compatible groupings frequent first, giving resolved summary. Two rules apply different conflict criteria rather frequency threshold. Loose() (semi-strict, combinable-component, consensus) keeps every grouping tree contradicts, MajorityPlus() keeps grouping displayed trees contradict . default Majority() threshold (p = 0.5) admits grouping seen exactly half trees, even half contradict , Majority() always subset MajorityPlus().
code: trees <- ape::read.tree(text = c(   "((((((t1,t3),t2),t8),(t5,t7)),t4),(t6,t9));",   "((((((t1,t3),(t5,t7)),t8),t2),t4),(t6,t9));",   "((((((t1,t2),t3),t8),(t5,t7)),t4),(t6,t9));",   "(((((t1,(t2,t3)),(t5,t7)),t8),t4),(t6,t9));",   "((((((t1,t2),t3),t8),(t4,(t5,t7))),t9),t6);",   "((((((t1,t3),t2),(t5,t7)),t8),t4),(t6,t9));",   "((((t1,((t2,t3),(t5,t7))),t8),(t6,t9)),t4);")) data.frame(   method  = c("Strict", "Majority", "Frequency", "Greedy"),   splits  = c(NSplits(Strict(trees)),    NSplits(Majority(trees)),               NSplits(Frequency(trees)),  NSplits(Greedy(trees))) ) #>      method splits #> 1    Strict      2 #> 2  Majority      4 #> 3 Frequency      5 #> 4    Greedy      6 oldPar <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(0.5, 0.5, 1.5, 0.5)) plot(Strict(trees),    main = "Strict") plot(Majority(trees),  main = "Majority-rule") plot(Frequency(trees), main = "Frequency difference") plot(Greedy(trees),    main = "Greedy (extended majority)") par(oldPar)

title: Summarizing tree samples with ConsTree
text: Adams(), RStar() Local() treat input rooted reason clusters rooted triplets rather unrooted splits. can therefore recover structure unrooted strict consensus collapses: Adams() may introduce groupings present input tree; RStar() keeps rooted triplet wins plurality alternatives; Local() returns minimum local consensus shared triplets (limited 20 leaves, best suited congruent samples).
code: rTrees <- ape::read.tree(text = c(   "((((t1,t2),t3),(((t4,t6),t8),t5)),t7);",   "(((((t1,t2),t3),(t4,(t6,t8))),t5),t7);",   "(((((t1,t2),t3),((t4,t8),t6)),t5),t7);",   "((((((t1,t2),t8),(t4,t6)),t3),t5),t7);",   "((((((t1,t2),t3),t8),(t4,t6)),t5),t7);",   "(((((t1,t2),t8),(t3,(t4,t6))),t5),t7);")) data.frame(   method = c("Strict", "Adams", "RStar", "Local"),   splits = c(NSplits(Strict(rTrees)), NSplits(Adams(rTrees)),              NSplits(RStar(rTrees)),  NSplits(Local(rTrees))) ) #>   method splits #> 1 Strict      1 #> 2  Adams      3 #> 3  RStar      4 #> 4  Local      3

title: Summarizing tree samples with ConsTree
text: second family ignores grouping frequencies instead seeks tree close sample chosen criterion. Quartet() finds approximate median minimizing total quartet distance inputs. quartet distance gives extra weight deep branches, result often resolved majority-rule tree: trees carry branch lengths, two summaries become available. Average() returns tree whose path-length (patristic) distances best match average input distance matrices, BHVMean() computes Fréchet mean Billera–Holmes–Vogtmann treespace, branch lengths; BHVDistance(), BHVPairwiseDistances() BHVVariance() provide underlying geodesic distances dispersion.

title: Summarizing tree samples with ConsTree
text: rule thumb: Strict() Loose() want well-supported groupings; Majority() familiar bootstrap summary; Frequency(), Greedy() Quartet() resolved picture; rooted methods root meaningful; Average() BHVMean() branch lengths matter. constructed consensus wanted, ‘TreeDist’ package offers complementary approach: can return sampled tree lowest median clustering-information distance rest – single representative sample rather summary built parts. ‘Rogue’ package can first strip unstable (‘rogue’) leaves, often sharpening consensus trees .

what: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: ‘ConsTree’ R package providing comprehensive, efficient suite methods summarizing collection phylogenetic trees — example bootstrap Bayesian posterior sample — single consensus tree. builds tree split infrastructure ‘TreeTools’, repackages family asymptotically-efficient consensus algorithms developed Jesper Jansson colleagues, several previously available R.

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: method takes list trees (multiPhylo) sharing leaves returns phylo. differ groupings (splits clusters) retain:

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: second family summarizes trees distance treespace criterion rather selecting groupings:

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
code: library("ConsTree")  trees <- ape::as.phylo(1:100, 8)   # 100 eight-leaf trees  Strict(trees)        # most conservative Majority(trees)      # the familiar 50% majority-rule tree Loose(trees)         # everything not actively contradicted Frequency(trees)     # frequency-difference: often more resolved than majority Greedy(trees)        # most resolved of the split-based summaries

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: Install development version GitHub: ‘ConsTree’ yet CRAN.
code: if (!require("remotes")) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ms609/ConsTree")

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: ‘ConsTree’ front-end consensus toolkit TreeTools ecosystem, also includes ‘TreeDist’ (tree distances information-theoretic consensus) ‘TreeSearch’ (phylogenetic search). TreeTools remains fast engine strict majority-rule consensus, ‘ConsTree’ exposes consistently-named wrappers. ‘TreeDist’ also offers complementary summary ‘ConsTree’ duplicate: tree sample lowest median clustering-information distance (CID) others — single representative sample rather constructed consensus. quartet machinery underlying Quartet() builds ‘Quartet’ package, counts resolved- shared-quartet statistics trees; BHV summaries relate ‘distory’, computes geodesic distances treespace. ‘Rogue’ package identifies unstable (‘rogue’) leaves whose removal can improve resolution support consensus tree; dropping rogues summarizing ‘ConsTree’ often yields better-resolved result.

title: An R package for fast phylogenetic consensus tree algorithms.
text: algorithms repackaged originate FACT, FACT2 FDCT prototypes Jesper Jansson colleagues, incorporated permission; see inst/REFERENCES.bib source papers, DESCRIPTION copyright attribution. ‘ConsTree’ released GPL (≥ 3). Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing, agree abide terms.

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
text: Adams() returns Adams consensus (Adams 1972) , summary set rooted trees.

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
code: Adams(trees)

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
text: trees list trees, multiPhylo object; entries must share leaf labels.

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
text: Adams() returns consensus tree, object class phylo, rooted construction.

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
text: Adams consensus defined recursively.  top level, leaves partitioned coarsest grouping every input tree refines: two leaves fall block , every input tree, sit child recent common ancestor full leaf set. block becomes child consensus root, construction repeats within block. Unlike split-based methods (Strict(), Majority(), Loose(), Greedy(), MajorityPlus(), Frequency()), Adams consensus can contain cluster appears individual input tree, depends input trees rooted: statement nesting, unrooted bipartitions.  Input trees treated rooted current root; root trees intend calling Adams().

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
text: Adams EN (1972). “Consensus techniques comparison taxonomic trees.” Systematic Zoology, 21(4), 390–397. doi:10.2307/2412432 .

title: Adams consensus tree — Adams
code: # Two rooted trees that disagree only on the position of one leaf trees <- c(ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, b), c), d);"),            ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, b), d), c);")) Adams(trees) # keeps the clade (a, b); leaves c, d unresolved at the root #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 4 tips and 2 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   a, b, c, d #>  #> Unrooted; no branch length.

title: Average consensus tree — Average
text: Average() returns average consensus (Lapointe Cucumel 1997) : tree whose path-length (patristic) distances closely match average path-length distances input trees.  Informally, places leaf mean position across input trees, making natural distance-based summary posterior sample – complementing split-based Strict() Majority() methods.

title: Average consensus tree — Average
code: Average(   trees,   method = c("fastme.bal", "ls", "nj", "bionj", "fastme.ols"),   weights = NULL,   scale = c("none", "max"),   edgeLengths = NA,   outgroup = NULL,   check.labels = TRUE,   lsControl = list() )

title: Average consensus tree — Average
text: trees list trees, multiPhylo object; entries must share leaf labels. method Character specifying build tree average distance matrix: "fastme.bal" (default) returns balanced minimum-evolution tree (Desper Gascuel 2002) : fast, accurate approximation least-squares tree; "ls" searches least-squares tree – criterion Lapointe & Cucumel's averaging guarantee holds – using TreeSearch::LeastSquaresTree(), compiled non-negative least-squares NNI/SPR search; "nj", "bionj" "fastme.ols" return corresponding distance tree (Saitou Nei 1987; Gascuel 1997) . weights Numeric vector specifying weight tree average (e.g. posterior probabilities), one entry per tree.  Defaults NULL, weights every tree equally – appropriate posterior sample, tree's frequency already encodes probability. scale Character specifying whether rescale tree's distance matrix averaging.  "none" (default) leaves matrices unscaled, appropriate trees already commensurable (e.g. single posterior sample).  "max" divides matrix largest entry, standardization recommended Lapointe & Cucumel combining trees heterogeneous sources whose absolute distances comparable. edgeLengths Logical specifying whether use branch lengths computing path-length distances.  default, NA, uses branch lengths every tree otherwise counts edges; TRUE requires branch lengths; FALSE always counts edges (topology-summary). outgroup Character vector specifying tip label(s) root result.  Defaults NULL, returns unrooted tree: path-length distances unaffected rooting, method intrinsically unrooted. check.labels Logical specifying whether confirm every tree describes leaves.  default, TRUE, safer; FALSE faster trees known share identical leaf set. lsControl Named list arguments least-squares search (method = "ls"), passed TreeSearch::LeastSquaresTree(); example list(spr = FALSE, maxHits = 5L, weight = "fm") use Fitch-Margoliash weighting.  Defaults list(); ignored methods.

title: Average consensus tree — Average
text: Average() returns average consensus tree, object class phylo fitted branch lengths, unrooted unless outgroup given.

title: Average consensus tree — Average
text: procedure two steps (Lapointe Cucumel 1997) : Compute path-length distance matrix input tree (using branch lengths present, otherwise counting edges), optionally rescaling matrix, average matrices. Find tree whose path-length distances best fit average matrix, least-squares sense. average several path-length matrices usually realisable tree (violates four-point condition), step 2 fit, inversion.  default Average() approximates fast balanced minimum-evolution tree; method = "ls" instead performs exact least-squares search, – NP-hard (Day 1987)  – uses tree rearrangements, original FITCH implementation.  Branch lengths fitted non-negative least squares, fitted distances realisable tree, criterion requires. lone input tree average: returned (unrooted unless outgroup given) without refitting, method, scale, weights edgeLengths effect.

title: Average consensus tree — Average
text: Day WHE (1987). “Computational complexity inferring phylogenies dissimilarity matrices.” Bulletin Mathematical Biology, 49(4), 461–467. doi:10.1007/BF02458863 . Desper R, Gascuel O (2002). “Fast accurate phylogeny reconstruction algorithms based minimum-evolution principle.” Journal Computational Biology, 9(5), 687–705. doi:10.1089/106652702761034136 . Gascuel O (1997). “BIONJ: improved version NJ algorithm based simple model sequence data.” Molecular Biology Evolution, 14(7), 685–695. doi:10.1093/oxfordjournals.molbev.a025808 . Lapointe F, Cucumel G (1997). “average consensus procedure: combination weighted trees containing identical overlapping sets taxa.” Systematic Biology, 46(2), 306–312. doi:10.1093/sysbio/46.2.306 . Saitou N, Nei M (1987). “neighbor-joining method: new method reconstructing phylogenetic trees.” Molecular Biology Evolution, 4(4), 406–425. doi:10.1093/oxfordjournals.molbev.a040454 .

title: Average consensus tree — Average
code: trees <- ape::rmtree(5, 8)         # five random eight-leaf trees Average(trees)                     # fast (balanced minimum evolution) default #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 8 tips and 6 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   t5, t6, t2, t4, t7, t3, ... #>  #> Unrooted; includes branch length(s). # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("TreeSearch", quietly = TRUE) &&     exists("LeastSquaresTree", where = asNamespace("TreeSearch"),            mode = "function")) {   Average(trees, method = "ls")    # faithful least-squares fit (slower) } #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 8 tips and 6 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   t5, t6, t4, t1, t8, t7, ... #>  #> Unrooted; includes branch length(s). # }

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
text: BHVDistance() returns geodesic distance phylogenetic trees edge lengths Billera-Holmes-Vogtmann (BHV) treespace (Billera et al. 2001) , computed polynomial-time GTP algorithm Owen Provan (2011) .

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
code: BHVDistance(tree1, tree2 = NULL)  BHVDist(tree1, tree2 = NULL)  BHV(tree1, tree2 = NULL)

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
text: tree1, tree2 phylo tree, multiPhylo object, list phylo trees, carrying edge.length.  trees across arguments must share leaf labels.  tree2 may omitted tree1 collection, case pairwise distances within tree1 returned.

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
text: tree1 tree2 single trees: single non-negative number. One argument single tree, collection: named numeric vector one entry per tree collection. tree2 omitted, tree1 tree2 collection: stats::dist object pairwise distances. tree1 tree2 different collections: numeric matrix rows corresponding tree1 columns tree2.

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
text: Billera LJ, Holmes SP, Vogtmann K (2001). “Geometry space phylogenetic trees.” Advances Applied Mathematics, 27(4), 733–767. doi:10.1006/aama.2001.0759 . Owen M, Provan JS (2011). “fast algorithm computing geodesic distances tree space.” IEEE/ACM Transactions Computational Biology Bioinformatics, 8(1), 2–13. doi:10.1109/TCBB.2010.3 .

title: Geodesic (BHV) distance between trees — BHVDistance
code: set.seed(2) AddEdgeLengths <- function(tree) { tree$edge.length <- runif(nrow(tree$edge)); tree } trees <- lapply(1:4, function(i) AddEdgeLengths(TreeTools::RandomTree(8, root = TRUE))) t1 <- trees[[1]]; t2 <- trees[[2]]  BHVDistance(t1, t2)               # scalar #> [1] 2.742226 BHVDistance(t1, trees)            # named vector #> [1] 0.000000 2.742226 3.088498 3.158640 BHVDistance(trees)                # dist (pairwise) #>          1        2        3 #> 2 2.742226                   #> 3 3.088498 3.094400          #> 4 3.158640 3.187792 3.344174 BHVDistance(trees[1:2], trees[3:4])  # matrix #>          [,1]     [,2] #> [1,] 3.088498 3.158640 #> [2,] 3.094400 3.187792

what: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
text: BHVMean() returns Fréchet (Karcher) mean set trees BHV treespace: tree minimizes sum squared geodesic distances sample (Brown Owen 2020) .  known closed form, approximated iterative law--large-numbers algorithm Sturm (2003)  Miller et al. (2015) : starting sample tree, step walks fraction \(1/(k+1)\) way along geodesic towards randomly chosen sample tree.

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
code: BHVMean(trees, tolerance = 1e-04, maxIter = 100000L, cauchyLength = 10L)  BHVVariance(trees, mean = NULL, type = c("average", "sum"))

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
text: trees list trees, multiPhylo object; entries must share leaf labels carry edge.length. tolerance Numeric convergence threshold, relative sample standard deviation: iteration stops cauchyLength consecutive steps move estimate less tolerance times sample standard deviation.  Smaller values give precise mean cost iterations. maxIter Integer specifying maximum number iterations. cauchyLength Integer specifying number consecutive small steps required declare convergence. mean Object class phylo specifying pre-computed mean tree; computed via BHVMean() NULL (default). type Character specifying whether return mean squared distance ("average", default) total squared distance ("sum").

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
text: BHVMean() returns mean tree, object class phylo, attributes iterations (number steps taken) converged.  step length shrinks \(1/(k+1)\), converged = TRUE indicates successive estimates stopped moving appreciably (stopping rule met maxIter), guaranteed bound distance exact mean; tighten tolerance greater precision. BHVVariance() returns single non-negative number.

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
text: BHVVariance() returns Fréchet variance: default mean squared geodesic distance sample mean, \((1/r)\sum_i d(\bar T, T_i)^2\); type = "sum", total \(\sum_i d(\bar T, T_i)^2\). mean "sticky": perturbing one sample tree need move , pulled towards lower-dimensional (less resolved) orthants, may unresolved even sample trees binary (Brown Owen 2020) .

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
text: Brown DG, Owen M (2020). “Mean variance phylogenetic trees.” Systematic Biology, 69(1), 139–154. doi:10.1093/sysbio/syz041 . Miller E, Owen M, Provan JS (2015). “Polyhedral computational geometry averaging metric phylogenetic trees.” Advances Applied Mathematics, 68, 51–91. doi:10.1016/j.aam.2015.04.002 . Sturm K (2003). “Probability measures metric spaces nonpositive curvature.” Heat Kernels Analysis Manifolds, Graphs, Metric Spaces, volume 338 Contemporary Mathematics, 357–390. doi:10.1090/conm/338/06080 .

title: Fréchet mean and variance in BHV treespace — BHVMean
code: set.seed(0) trees <- lapply(1:25, function(i) {   tree <- TreeTools::RandomTree(6, root = FALSE)   tree$edge.length <- runif(nrow(tree$edge))   tree }) meanTree <- BHVMean(trees) BHVVariance(trees, mean = meanTree) #> [1] 1.254778

what: Fast algorithms for phylogenetic consensus trees — ConsTree-package
title: Fast algorithms for phylogenetic consensus trees — ConsTree-package
text: ConsTree provides comprehensive efficient suite methods summarizing set phylogenetic trees single consensus tree. builds tree split infrastructure TreeTools, implements asymptotically efficient consensus algorithms (Jansson et al. 2016) .

title: Fast algorithms for phylogenetic consensus trees — ConsTree-package
text: Maintainer: Martin R. Smith martin.smith@durham.ac.uk (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Martin R. Smith martin.smith@durham.ac.uk (ORCID) [copyright holder] contributors: Jesper Jansson (Consensus algorithms (FACT, FACT2, FDCT)) [copyright holder] Chuanqi Shen (Consensus algorithms (FACT)) [copyright holder] Wing-Kin Sung (Consensus algorithms (FACT, FACT2, FDCT)) [copyright holder]

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
text: Frequency() returns frequency-difference consensus: split retained occurs strictly often every split conflicts . Equivalently, among set mutually incompatible splits, consensus keeps split strictly frequent rivals.

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
code: Frequency(trees)

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
text: trees list trees, multiPhylo object; entries must share leaf labels.

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
text: Frequency() returns consensus tree, object class phylo, rooted first entry trees.

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
text: frequency-difference consensus least resolved majority-rule consensus (Majority()) – split half trees necessarily frequent conflicting split – contained within greedy consensus (Greedy()).  retained splits mutually compatible, define valid tree. efficient algorithm constructing consensus given (Jansson et al. 2024) ; present implementation computes tree directly pooled split frequencies.

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
text: Jansson J, Sung W, Tabatabaee SA, Yang Y (2024). “Faster Algorithm Constructing Frequency Difference Consensus Tree.” Beyersdorff O, Kanté MM, Kupferman O, Lokshtanov D (eds.), 41st International Symposium Theoretical Aspects Computer Science (STACS 2024), volume 289 Leibniz International Proceedings Informatics (LIPIcs), 43:1–43:17. doi:10.4230/LIPIcs.STACS.2024.43 .

title: Frequency-difference consensus tree — Frequency
code: trees <- ape::as.phylo(0:5, 8) Frequency(trees) #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 8 tips and 6 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, ... #>  #> Rooted; no branch length.

title: Greedy (extended majority-rule) consensus tree — Greedy

title: Local consensus tree — Local
text: local consensus conservative tree consistent rooted triplets shared every input tree.  Two variants offered: minimum rooted local consensus (MinRLC, type = "rooted") minimum induced local consensus (MinILC, type = "induced"), differ resolution result scored.  tree assembled Aho-graph decomposition common-triplet set dynamic programming subsets leaves. Complexity note: algorithm exponential, Local() limited n <= 20 leaves.  Running time depends n congruent input trees : rooted triplets shared (highly incongruent trees, particularly type = "induced"), dynamic programming can become intractable even within 20-leaf limit.  long-running call can interrupted (e.g. Ctrl-C). -valid-consensus: entire leaf set forms single inseparable Aho-graph component (common triplets separate pair), algorithm valid consensus; Local() returns star tree (leaves attached directly root, fully unresolved).  reference binary reports "valid consensus found." case. Input trees treated rooted current root; root trees intend calling Local().

title: Local consensus tree — Local
code: # Two trees that agree on one cherry but disagree on overall topology t1 <- ape::read.tree(text = "(1,((2,3),4));") t2 <- ape::read.tree(text = "(1,((2,4),3));") Local(list(t1, t2), "rooted")  # keeps clade {2,3,4} only #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 4 tips and 2 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   1, 2, 3, 4 #>  #> Rooted; no branch length.

what: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
text: Construct consensus tree minimizes sum symmetric quartet distances set input trees, using greedy add--prune heuristic.

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
code: Quartet(   trees,   init = c("majority", "empty", "extended"),   greedy = c("best", "first") )

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
text: trees Object class multiPhylo specifying input trees. trees must share tip labels. Trees may non-binary (polytomies handled correctly). init Character string specifying initial tree: "majority" (default): start majority-rule consensus. "empty": start star tree (purely additive). "extended": start extended (greedy) majority-rule consensus. greedy Character string specifying greedy strategy: "best" (default): evaluate candidates pick single highest-benefit action step. "first": pick first improving action encountered (faster may give slightly worse result).

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
text: Quartet() returns consensus tree, object class phylo, unrooted.

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
text: majority-rule consensus minimizes sum Robinson-Foulds distances input trees.  Analogously, Quartet() finds approximate median tree symmetric quartet distance (Takazawa et al. 2026) , counts false-positive false-negative resolved quartets equally. quartet distance gives greater weight deep branches (resolve quartets), quartet consensus trees tend resolved majority-rule trees, especially phylogenetic signal low. algorithm pools splits observed across input trees maintains quartet profile: \(\binom{n}{4}\) quartets, count many input trees resolve three possible topologies.  Splits greedily added (removed ) consensus reduces total symmetric quartet distance input trees.  Candidate splits must compatible currently included splits. function supports trees 100 tips.  larger trees, explicit quartet enumeration becomes prohibitively expensive.

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
text: Takazawa Y, Takeda , Hayamizu M, Gascuel O (2026). “Outperforming majority-rule consensus tree using fine-grained dissimilarity measures.” bioRxiv. doi:10.64898/2026.03.16.712085 .

title: Consensus tree minimizing quartet distance — Quartet
code: library(TreeTools) #> Loading required package: ape  # Generate bootstrap-like trees trees <- as.phylo(1:30, nTip = 8)  # Quartet consensus qc <- Quartet(trees) plot(qc)   # Compare resolution with majority-rule mr <- UnrootTree(Consensus(trees, p = 0.5)) cat("Majority-rule splits:", NSplits(mr), "\n") #> Majority-rule splits: 2  cat("Quartet consensus splits:", NSplits(qc), "\n") #> Quartet consensus splits: 3

title: R* consensus tree — RStar

title: R* consensus tree — RStar
text: R* consensus rooted-triplet method.  every set three leaves tallies, across input trees, three possible resolved rooted triplets (ab|c, ac|b, bc|) keeps one uniquely favoured: resolution appearing strictly input trees two, considered separately.  strict plurality rule, majority rule: triplet can uniquely favoured far fewer half votes (e.g. 2–1–1 split among four trees), tie leaves taxa unresolved.  kept triplets form set majority resolved triplets, \(R_{maj}\).  R* tree unique tree whose clades exactly strong clusters \(R_{maj}\) (Degnan et al. 2009; Jansson et al. 2016) : leaf set clade , every pair leaves every leaf x outside , triplet grouping pair x uniquely favoured. Equivalently, R* resolved tree displays resolved triplet outside \(R_{maj}\). R* always refinement majority-rule consensus (every majority clade also appears RStar()) statistically consistent estimator species tree gene trees.  Unlike Local() polynomial, restricted small leaf counts; RStar() caps n 200 purely memory safeguard dense triplet tensor (limit quite different nature Local()'s exact-exponential 20-leaf bound). Like Adams(), R* rooted method: triplet states depend rooting, input trees treated rooted current root.  Root trees intend calling RStar().

title: R* consensus tree — RStar
text: Fans.  non-binary input tree leaves three leaves unresolved (fan), tree count toward resolution triplet; fans impact \(R_{maj}\) (Jansson et al. 2016) . Ties.  resolution triple uniquely favoured, triple contributes nothing, leaving affected taxa unresolved. Existence uniqueness.  strong-cluster construction always yields single, well-defined tree (Lemma 1.1 (Jansson et al. 2016) ); incompatibility "build-failure" case resolve. Algorithm.  implementation correctness-first: \(O(kn^3)\) triplet tally followed \(O(n^4)\) strong-cluster assembly.  sub-cubic near-quadratic algorithms (Jansson Sung 2013; Jansson et al. 2016)  deferred speed optimisation.

title: R* consensus tree — RStar
text: Degnan JH, DeGiorgio M, Bryant D, Rosenberg NA (2009). “Properties consensus methods inferring species trees gene trees.” Systematic Biology, 58(1), 35–54. doi:10.1093/sysbio/syp008 . Jansson J, Sung W (2013). “Constructing R* consensus tree two trees subcubic time.” Algorithmica, 66(2), 329–345. doi:10.1007/s00453-012-9639-1 . Jansson J, Sung W, Vu H, Yiu S (2016). “Faster algorithms computing R* consensus tree.” Algorithmica, 76(4), 1224–1244. doi:10.1007/s00453-016-0122-2 .

title: R* consensus tree — RStar
code: # Five trees whose majority signal recovers the species tree (((a,b),c),d): trees <- c(   ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, b), c), d);"),   ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, b), c), d);"),   ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, b), c), d);"),   ape::read.tree(text = "(((a, c), b), d);"),   ape::read.tree(text = "(((b, c), a), d);") ) RStar(trees) # (a, b) wins {a,b,c} by plurality (3 vs 1 vs 1) #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 4 tips and 3 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   d, c, a, b #>  #> Rooted; no branch length.

title: ConsTree 1.0.0 (2026-06-01)
text: First public release: consensus-tree toolkit built TreeTools. Split-based consensus methods: Strict(), Majority() / MajorityRule(): wrappers around TreeTools::Consensus(). Loose(): loose (semi-strict / combinable-component) consensus. Greedy(): greedy (extended majority-rule) consensus. MajorityPlus(): majority-rule (+) consensus. Frequency(): frequency-difference consensus. Rooted-tree consensus methods: Adams(): Adams consensus. RStar(): R* consensus, assembled strong clusters majority resolved-triplet set (Jansson, Sung, Vu & Yiu 2016). Local(): local consensus (MinRLC / MinILC). Distance- branch-length-based summaries: Average(): distance-based average consensus. Quartet(): tree minimizing summed symmetric quartet distance inputs (Takazawa et al. 2026). Billera-Holmes-Vogtmann treespace summaries: BHVDistance() (Owen-Provan geodesic distance), BHVPairwiseDistances(), BHVMean() (Fréchet mean) BHVVariance(). introductory vignette (ConsTree) demonstrates method family worked exemplars.
